summary: Not applicable. Alfred is a terminal text editor with no HTML, web content, or UI rendered through web standards. The TUI is built with crossterm and ratatui (terminal rendering libraries). WCAG accessibility standards do not apply to terminal applications. Accessibility concerns for terminal editors are platform-specific (terminal emulator support, screen reader integration via JAWS/NVDA).

recommendations.title: Document terminal accessibility support
recommendations.description: Create ACCESSIBILITY.md documenting: (1) screen reader support via terminal accessibility APIs, (2) keyboard-only navigation (already vim-style), (3) color contrast in default theme, (4) tested terminal emulators.

recommendations.title: Test with NVDA and JAWS on Windows, VoiceOver on macOS
recommendations.description: Coordinate with accessibility testers to validate terminal output is readable by assistive technologies. Test arrow key navigation, command entry, and status line announcements.

recommendations.title: Ensure status bar and messages use semantic positioning
recommendations.description: Terminal screen readers may require explicit cursor positioning or ANSI escape sequences for semantic regions. Verify status bar position is announced correctly.
